creator: michael.hmich@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to martin from comment #33)


> This is the correct syntax for fstab:
> 
> /dev/ada0s1             /Linux_Mint     ext2fs  rw,noauto  0       0
> /dev/ada0s4             /Arch           ext2fs  rw,noauto  0       0
> 
> This works for me.

so that's not funny, because it works now here too
before "FreeBSD 14.0-STABLE #6 stable/14-n265655-859d674e1745: Fri Oct 27 02:41:11 -03 2023" it wasn't, as I posted several times before

it is still not working with only "noauto" with the same error

fstab man about 
     The fourth field, (fs_mntops), describes the mount options associated
     with the file system.  It is formatted as a comma separated list of
     options.  It contains at least the type of mount (see fs_type below) plus
     any additional options appropriate to the file system type. 

but fs_type
    just talks about swap and md

ext2fs man
    says nothing about and example suggests the rw is automatic 
    as the example shows it also works
         To mount a ext2fs volume located on /dev/ada1s1:
           mount -t ext2fs /dev/ada1s1 /mnt

    as reported earlier, it really worked


then still is there the s1/p1 confusion and apparently not addressed outside the knowledge of some 


> Werner Losch comment 29
> Did you ever upload the first MB or so of the disk? 
> I think the EBR code is getting the number of valid 
> partitions wrong so bogusly checking 33...

no I haven't, is it still required?
if so I could do it tomorrow morning
